segments.text: You are the children of the Lord your God.
Do not cut yourselves or shave the front of your heads for the dead.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For you are a people holy to the Lord your God. Out of all the peoples on the face of the earth, the Lord has chosen you to be his treasured possession.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Do not eat any detestable thing.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: These are the animals you may eat: the ox, the sheep, the goat,
the deer, the gazelle, the roe deer, the wild goat, the ibex, the antelope and the mountain sheep.
You may eat any animal that has a divided hoof and that chews the cud.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: However, of those that chew the cud or that have a divided hoof you may not eat the camel, the rabbit or the hyrax. Although they chew the cud, they do not have a divided hoof; they are ceremonially unclean for you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The pig is also unclean; although it has a divided hoof, it does not chew the cud. You are not to eat their meat or touch their carcasses.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Of all the creatures living in the water, you may eat any that has fins and scales.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But anything that does not have fins and scales you may not eat; for you it is unclean.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You may eat any clean bird.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But these you may not eat: the eagle, the vulture, the black vulture,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: the red kite, the black kite, any kind of falcon,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: any kind of raven,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: the horned owl, the screech owl, the gull, any kind of hawk,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: the little owl, the great owl, the white owl,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: the desert owl, the osprey, the cormorant,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: the stork, any kind of heron, the hoopoe and the bat.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: All flying insects are unclean to you; do not eat them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But any winged creature that is clean you may eat.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Do not eat anything you find already dead. You may give it to the foreigner residing in any of your towns, and they may eat it, or you may sell it to any other foreigner. But you are a people holy to the Lord your God. Do not cook a young goat in its mother's milk.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Be sure to set aside a tenth of all that your fields produce each year.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Eat the tithe of your grain, new wine and olive oil, and the firstborn of your herds and flocks in the presence of the Lord your God at the place he will choose as a dwelling for his Name, so that you may learn to revere the Lord your God always.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But if that place is too distant and you have been blessed by the Lord your God and cannot carry your tithe (because the place where the Lord will choose to put his Name is so far away),
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then exchange your tithe for silver, and take the silver with you and go to the place the Lord your God will choose.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Use the silver to buy whatever you like: cattle, sheep, wine or other fermented drink, or anything you wish. Then you and your household shall eat there in the presence of the Lord your God and rejoice.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And do not neglect the Levites living in your towns, for they have no allotment or inheritance of their own.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: At the end of every three years, bring all the tithes of that year's produce and store it in your towns,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So that the Levites (who have no allotment or inheritance of their own) and the foreigners, the fatherless and the widows who live in your towns may come and eat and be satisfied, and so that the Lord your God may bless you in all the work of your hands.
segments.speaker: God
